clinical_history: A middle-aged man was found at home in a confused mental state. The ECG strongly suggests what abnormality?
diagnosis: The patient had systemic hypothermia with a rectal temperature of 30.6˚C, associated with pneumonia and sepsis.  The ECG shows marked sinus bradycardia (35/min) with classic Osborn (J) waves. The PR is prolonged (280msc). The QT/QTc is also prolonged (about 510 msec/506 msec, respectively, using Hodges formula for rate correction). The QRS duration (not including the J wave) appears normal. QRS voltage is also within normal limits.  Note the baseline artifact, which may be due to shivering, a common finding in this context. ~~~~Blood chemistries showed glucose:74 mg/dL, calcium: 9.1 mg/dL and potassium: 3.9 mEq/L. TSH was within normal limits at 4.2 microIU/mL.  ~~~~Please see Case 479 for further discussion of the basis of pathologic J waves and concomitant findings with systemic hypothermia.~~
